arguments.task: Research Notion using official and first-party sources only where possible. Visit developers.notion.com to identify API surface and auth model. Then find official Notion pages for authorization, capabilities/reference, request limits, and pricing/access relevant to API use. Also check the official Notion MCP documentation if available. Return a concise evidence-backed summary with exact source URLs and quotes/snippets for: API type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), auth methods, whether access is self-serve or gated, rate limit notes, and whether an official MCP exists.

input.thought: Open official Notion sources and extract evidence for API, auth, access, rate limits, and MCP.

arguments.task: Using official Notion pages only, extract exact evidence with URLs for these fields: 1) API type and breadth from overview/reference pages, 2) auth methods from authorization page, 3) rate limits from request limits page, 4) whether an official Notion MCP exists from MCP overview, and 5) whether API access is self-serve or gated from any official docs/pricing/setup pages. Return the final answer as structured bullet points with direct source URLs and short quoted snippets.

input.thought: Run a narrower extraction to get final structured evidence from official pages.

raw_result.description: Workspace platform for docs, databases, and collaboration with a public developer API for Notion content and integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe OAuth 2.0 for public integrations and bearer tokens for internal integrations via an integration token; docs also mention personal access via internal-style setup. Exact schema for PAT terminology may need human confirmation.
raw_result.gating_reason: Notion’s developer docs provide self-serve integration creation and authorization flows without contact-sales or partner approval requirements.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with reference endpoints for blocks, pages, databases/data sources, search, comments, users, and authentication-related integration flows; sufficient for a first agent toolkit but scoped to Notion workspace/content operations rather than a broad platform surface.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Notion developer docs include a hosted Notion MCP server overview and setup guidance.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official request limits page states an average rate limit of 3 requests per second, with some burst allowance; limits are enforced per integration, and 429 responses may include a Retry-After header.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs explicitly cover OAuth setup, internal integrations, a full API reference, request limits, and an official hosted MCP server—strong signs of current agent-toolkit readiness.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Pricing/access evidence was inferred from self-serve developer setup rather than a dedicated official pricing/access page in the gathered evidence, and token taxonomy (integration token vs personal access token wording) should be verified by a human if strict labeling matters.

result.auth_notes: Official docs describe OAuth 2.0 for public integrations and bearer tokens for internal integrations via an integration token; docs also mention personal access via internal-style setup. Exact schema for PAT terminology may need human confirmation.
result.gating_reason: Notion’s developer docs provide self-serve integration creation and authorization flows without contact-sales or partner approval requirements.

api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Notion developer docs include a hosted Notion MCP server overview and setup guidance.
